required_contains.text: The documentation assigns those per-subnet symbols in Greek-alphabet order. It refers abstractly to
subnet α's token as α, subnet β's as β, subnet γ's as γ, and so on, while command-line examples show
concrete rows such as netuid 1 with an alpha symbol, netuid 2 with beta, netuid 3 with gamma, and
netuid 9 with iota in `btcli subnet list` output. TAO uses τ (tau), and root-subnet examples use
capital Τ because that subnet has no separate alpha currency
